scope: Next highest unprojected returners/transfers by historical anchor, with role scores gated by projected-lineup or roster-role evidence.
method: Prioritize the top unprojected players by historical rating and likely leaderboard impact.
Only assign projected starter credit when a projected lineup, school roster/starter history, or explicit role context supports it.
Keep high historical anchors from falling too far when role is safe, but do not inflate transfers entering crowded high-major rosters.
Use production and awards for talent/context, but keep projectedRole tied to minutes and team hierarchy.
Leave players in review state so the admin board can apply or reject the researched projection.

formulas.projectedBbprWithHistory: historicalRating/proven production blended with projection score, then calibrated by player type

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Yes requires lineup/start-history evidence; Uncertain means likely minutes without a clean starter signal; No means bench/rotation projection.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: 90+ for clear starters/primary options, 82-89 for likely starters or strong rotation players, 72-81 for rotation players with moderate/crowded roles.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Rewards clean depth-chart openings and returning usage; penalizes crowded rotations.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Primary creator/scorer roles get 86+; rim runners, defensive bigs, and connective guards are lower.
teamContextRubric.roleEvidence: Every player includes a note explaining whether starter credit comes from projected lineups, starter history, or roster inference.

players.researchSummary: Defty has one of the strongest remaining historical anchors and current Arizona State coverage expects him to start at center. Role is high because of rim protection/frontcourt need, while offensive burden is capped.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Arizona State coverage says it is difficult to believe Defty will not be the starting center.
teamContext.roleCap: Starter center, not primary offensive option.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Frontcourt partner Paulius Murauskas raises lineup quality but shares usage.

evidence.notes: Projects Defty as the likely starting center and a defensive rim-protection fit.

players.researchSummary: Holloway has clean projected-starter evidence for Alabama and should be one of the Tide's lead guards. His BBPR is lifted by role and shot creation, but remains anchored below the elite returners.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Rothstein projects Holloway in Alabama's starting five.
teamContext.roleCap: High-major starting guard with real usage, but shared offense with Allen, Jemison, Fielder, and Garrison.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Alabama has freshman and transfer depth behind him.

evidence.notes: Projects Holloway as an Alabama starting guard.

players.researchSummary: Francis is Rutgers' proven lead scorer and returns with All-Big Ten honorable mention production. Starter/role are high from production and team hierarchy, but NBA/upside stays moderate.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Rutgers roster bio frames Francis as a returning dynamic scorer after leading the team at 17.0 PPG.
teamContext.roleCap: Primary college guard, not elite NBA prospect.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Rutgers has guards around him, but Francis owns the clearest scoring role.

evidence.notes: Francis returns after leading Rutgers with 17.0 PPG and earning All-Big Ten honorable mention.

players.researchSummary: Cain has strong starter evidence: 74 straight starts, returning starter context, and top-two team production. He gets major role credit, but the final score stays in a proven-starter tier rather than a star/upside tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Georgia says Cain has started 74 consecutive games and returns for 2026-27.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Georgia returns multiple starters, so burden is shared.

evidence.notes: Cain returns after ranking second on Georgia in points, rebounds, assists, and steals.
Georgia is returning four starters including Cain.

players.researchSummary: Punch was a bit low relative to his Texas frontcourt role, Big 12 production, and defensive value. The correction moves him into a stronger high-major starter band.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Punch was a bit low relative to his Texas frontcourt role, Big 12 production, and defensive value. The correction moves him into a stronger high-major starter band.
teamContext.roleCap: Corrected so role, anchor, and current trajectory are comparable across the full rated pool.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Texas frontcourt/wing depth lowers offensive burden.
teamContext.reassessment: July 22 reassessment lifted this row after player-specific role/production research showed the prior historical/role anchor was too conservative.
teamContext.scoredAnchor: Synthetic historical anchor: 79. Updated in the July 23 top-100 calibration pass.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Punch's Texas fit.

players.researchSummary: High Point featured star after second-team All-CAA and CAA All-Defensive production at Monmouth: 15.8 PPG, 8.1 RPG, 2.9 APG, 2.0 SPG.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Starter inferred from transfer production and High Point roster need, not from a definitive projected lineup.
teamContext.roleCap: Large mid-major role with modest NBA/upside.
teamContext.positionCompetition: High Point's transfer-heavy roster adds uncertainty.
teamContext.reassessment: July 22 reassessment lifted this row after player-specific role/production research showed the prior historical/role anchor was too conservative.

evidence.notes: Confirms Rivera-Torres on the High Point roster.

players.researchSummary: Fru has a useful developmental profile, but Marquette frontcourt starter evidence is not strong enough to give him a big role score. He should be treated as a rotation frontcourt player unless lineup reporting improves.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Roster placement/fit, not clean starter evidence.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Marquette's frontcourt additions cap early opportunity.

players.researchSummary: Reassessed against Tomislav: Zvonimir's counting stats trail, but the original model/team-impact read is stronger because of rim protection, block rate, floor-spacing flashes, and Illinois' ability to use him as a higher-impact two-way frontcourt piece.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Illinois roster/projection context supports frontcourt minutes, but Mirkovic is the higher-usage piece.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely frontcourt starter/major rotation, not primary option.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Illinois has multiple frontcourt and wing pieces.

evidence.notes: Lists Zvonimir on the 2026-27 Illinois roster and documents 6.6 PPG, 4.6 RPG, 2.0 BPG, fourth nationally in block rate, and made threes in 16 games in 2025-26.

players.researchSummary: Hall receives a development/role bump as a sophomore returning creator with a strong star-score profile. Starter/usage are strong by roster inference, but confidence is below A because projected-lineup evidence is lighter.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Returning production and roster role inference, not a definitive projected lineup.
teamContext.positionCompetition: New Mexico roster turnover supports opportunity.

evidence.notes: Confirms Hall on New Mexico's roster.

players.researchSummary: Domon gets a meaningful sophomore development bump and likely major Murray State role. The final score stays below the stronger high-major returners due competition level and limited national upside signal.

teamContext.positionCompetition: Opportunity appears clean but national evidence is limited.

evidence.notes: Confirms Domon on Murray State's roster.

players.researchSummary: Davis should be in Creighton's guard mix, but starter credit is not clean enough yet. The projection gives transfer-rotation credit without pushing him into the proven-starter band.
Dent projects as a key Saint Mary's guard with returning continuity. Role is strong, while final score is kept anchored because national upside evidence is moderate.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Saint Mary's roster continuity and guard role inference.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Gaels guard rotation creates shared burden.

evidence.notes: Confirms Dent on Saint Mary's roster.

players.researchSummary: Archie has transfer-rotation upside, but Texas A&M starter evidence is not firm enough to give him a major role bump. Keep him near his historical anchor pending clearer lineup reports.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Roster addition and production fit, not confirmed starter.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Texas A&M has a crowded transfer guard group.

evidence.notes: Confirms Archie on Texas A&M's roster.

players.researchSummary: Fogle gets a sophomore development bump and Gonzaga rotation upside, but starter credit is not clean in a roster still sorting guard/wing minutes.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Returning Gonzaga role/roster inference, not confirmed starter.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Gonzaga's updated lineup has unsettled perimeter roles.

evidence.notes: Gonzaga projected-lineup context shows unresolved guard/wing hierarchy.
